message: auth-api canary for DEP-506 is green in us-east. Rolling production in 5 unless anyone sees SSO smoke failures.
I ran the Okta and AzureAD happy path against staging after PR 206. Worth watching vanity domains because the callback path changed.
DEP-506 completed: auth-api@2026.05.20.1 to production, related_pr=206, deployer=maya.
API gateway p95 for /oauth/callback moved from 280ms to about 850ms. Not paging yet but that is a sharp slope.
Web DEP-507 is out too. Account settings only, no login code touched.
Seeing auth-api CPU up 18 percent after DEP-506. Could be extra redirect parsing? DB pool looks normal.
First enterprise login ticket just landed from Northstar. Users get bounced back to IdP then see invalid_redirect_uri.
Checking callback normalization. The error is before session exchange, so password auth should be unaffected.
Sentry issue AUTH-CALLBACK-219 went from 0 to 63 events in 10m. Mostly enterprise tenants with custom domains.
Cloudlane and Finch Capital now reporting SSO loops. Support macro says status page not updated yet.
Declaring INC-334 for enterprise SSO failures. IC=sam, tech lead=maya, comms=jamie.
Current signal: started after DEP-506 at 09:14Z, p95 auth callback latency up, invalid_redirect_uri errors, customer reports from Northstar/Cloudlane/Finch.
Looking at PR 206 diff. normalizeRedirectUri lowercases host and strips trailing slash before allowlist check.
Frontend deploy is not touching /login or callback. I can reproduce with direct IdP initiated SSO, so browser bundle seems out.
Zendesk volume is climbing: 8 tickets in 20 minutes, all enterprise SSO, admins can still log in with password fallback if enabled.
DEP-508 billing export is deploying. Unrelated service, but calling it out because the incident timeline is noisy.
Hypothesis: tenant allowlist stores configured ACS URL with mixed-case vanity domain. We canonicalize inbound but compare to raw config.
Auth-api error rate is 7.8 percent overall, but /sso/callback is 42 percent for enterprise tenants. Consumer login steady.
Severity set high. Customer impact is enterprise SSO only, about 31 workspaces with failed callbacks since 09:37Z.
Can confirm with Northstar config: stored callback has https://Login.Northstar.example.com/sso/callback/ and request normalizes to lowercase no slash.
NovaGrid exec sponsor is asking for ETA. They have a board meeting and nobody can get into analytics.
Fast fix is compare canonical-to-canonical. Safer rollback is revert PR 206 because relay state validation PR 210 has not deployed yet.
Holding further auth-api deploys unless incident channel approves. DEP-509 web is complete and unrelated.
Latency keeps climbing because retries are hammering callback. Rate limiting IdP retry loops would reduce noise but not fix login.
No DB regression. Failed requests do not reach session insert. This points squarely at callback validation.
Status page draft: enterprise SSO login failures for some customers. Password auth and API tokens unaffected.
Decision point in 10m: rollback DEP-506 vs hotfix canonical compare. Biasing rollback unless hotfix is already reviewed.
DEP-510 SAML relay state validation was already queued and just went out. It does not touch redirect URI normalization path.
Hotfix has tests but I do not love shipping it under pressure. Revert PR 206 is four files and restores prior callback behavior.
Approve rollback path. Maya please prep revert PR, Priya review, Erin watch auth callback error rate.
Customer count now 19 tickets, worst affected: Northstar, Cloudlane, Finch, NovaGrid, AtlasWorks.
Revert PR is up as 212. It only backs out normalizeRedirectUri and leaves relay_state validation intact.
Reviewed PR 212. The old allowlist compare is ugly but known good. Added a follow-up test note for vanity domains.
Posting status page update now. Wording says identified cause and preparing rollback.
Sentry now at 612 AUTH-CALLBACK-219 events since start. Peak is 74/min at 11:31Z.
PR 212 merged to main. Waiting for auth-api production deploy approval.
Rollback approved. Deploying DEP-512 to production. Freeze still applies to auth-api after rollback.
DEP-512 canary has 0 invalid_redirect_uri in 6 minutes. Promoting to full prod.
Callback p95 dropped from 2.8s to 410ms on canary slice. Full fleet still draining retries.
DEP-512 completed: auth-api@2026.05.20.7 rollback fix deployed to production.
Northstar confirmed new SSO sessions are working. Asking Cloudlane and Finch to retry.
AUTH-CALLBACK-219 is back to baseline. Remaining errors are stale IdP retries with old relay state.
Resolving INC-334. Root cause: PR 206 canonicalized inbound callback URLs but compared against non-canonical tenant allowlist entries.
Action item: tenant-aware canonical allowlist helper and regression tests for mixed-case vanity domains plus trailing slash.
Ticket cleanup: 27 total customer contacts tied to INC-334. Four asked for RCA, two asked for SLA credit language.
Draft RCA notes: why staging missed it = fixtures only used lowercase callback URLs and no vanity domain with trailing slash.
DEP-513 completed: auth-api regression tests deployed, no runtime behavior change.
Post-incident review scheduled for tomorrow. Please add notes before EOD, especially any pre-rollback observations.
Adding temporary Sentry sample bump for auth callback so we can verify there is no quiet tail.
DEP-514 completed: auth-api Sentry sampling increased for callback errors.
Cloudlane confirmed recovery. They want a timestamped RCA because their admins lost about 90 minutes of onboarding time.
Leaving note: billing export queue incident was separate and started after DEP-508. Do not correlate it with SSO failures.
INC-334 final impact estimate: 43 enterprise workspaces had failed SSO attempts, 31 opened sessions after rollback.
PR 216 password reset parser is unrelated to callback code. Mentioning because it is another auth-api deploy today.
RCA requests are tagged incident_inc_334 and linked to impacted deployments DEP-506 and DEP-512.
No residual client-side login errors in web telemetry. The spike was server callback validation.
Heads up, separate webhooks incident INC-337 after DEP-517. Different service and symptoms.
Morning check: auth callback errors stayed baseline overnight after rollback.
RCA draft has customer timeline: first ticket 09:39Z, status page 11:30Z, recovery 12:47Z.
Root cause fix PR 226 will canonicalize both sides and migrate stored allowlist entries. Keeping it behind auth.ssoCanonicalCompare.
